message:
perf(search): add full-field JSON fast-path bypassing serde_json::Value intermediary

Add an early-return fast path in output_robot_results for the common case
of full-field JSON output with no optional metadata (no truncation,
max_tokens, meta, warnings, aggregations, suggestions, explanations, or
timeouts). Instead of serializing every hit to serde_json::Value and then
re-serializing, this path:

- Defines FullHitCompat implementing custom Serialize to emit only the
  12-14 fields (workspace_original and origin_host are conditional) via
  serialize_map, avoiding Value allocation entirely
- Constructs a FastJsonPayload struct with #[serde(serialize_with)]
  for the hits array, streaming directly to BufWriter<stdout>
- Uses serde_json::to_writer_pretty for zero-copy output

This eliminates the Value intermediary for the majority of --robot JSON
queries where no special features are active, reducing per-hit allocation
and improving throughput for large result sets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
